Arundo donax, commonly known as the giant cane, is a type of perennial grass that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Arundo donax include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Arundo donax include teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as topical applications such as creams and salves, which are often used to treat wounds and skin conditions.
